cast_history.note: built-in/stock tool branch via galaxy-tool-cache bare-id flow

refs.purpose: Validate the mutated draft against draft-contract rules; with --concrete, also gate the extracted concrete subset (including the step just implemented) against full gxformat2.
refs.trigger: After implementing or modifying a concrete tool step in the draft.
refs.verification: Cast the skill, exercise on an IWC-derived draft with one drafty step, confirm both surfaces validate and diagnostics route back to the implemented step.

refs.purpose: Implement identifier-derived collection reshaping via Apply Rules.
refs.trigger: When collection element identifiers need regex parsing, nesting-level swaps, regrouping, or paired identifier assignment.

refs.purpose: Connect concrete Galaxy tool inputs/outputs while preserving collection mapping and reduction semantics.
refs.trigger: When implementing a step with data_collection inputs, mapped outputs, reductions, or nested collection wiring.

refs.purpose: Connect concrete Galaxy tool inputs/outputs while preserving collection mapping and reduction semantics.
refs.trigger: When implementing a step with data_collection inputs, mapped outputs, reductions, or nested collection wiring.

refs.purpose: Connect concrete Galaxy tool inputs/outputs while preserving collection mapping and reduction semantics.
refs.trigger: When implementing a step with data_collection inputs, mapped outputs, reductions, or nested collection wiring.

refs.purpose: Insert built-in Galaxy collection-operation steps when a direct tool connection cannot express the needed shape.
refs.trigger: When a step needs collection construction, filtering, extraction, zipping, unzipping, flattening, merging, or relabeling.

refs.purpose: Preserve concrete tool/job failure evidence while implementing step labels, tool ids, output labels, and collection wiring.
refs.trigger: When a selected wrapper has explicit failure semantics, dynamic outputs, non-default stdio rules, strict-shell behavior, or runtime-only failure risk.

refs.purpose: Preserve testable output labels and collection element identifiers while replacing abstract steps with concrete gxformat2 steps.
refs.trigger: When a concrete step changes output labels, emits collection outputs, creates a diagnostic checkpoint, or makes a final output too weakly assertable.

refs.purpose: Turn operator-derived abstract transforms into concrete Galaxy wiring, collection operations, or review requests.
refs.trigger: When a concrete step implements behavior traced to map, join, groupTuple, branch, mix, combine, or multiMap.

refs.purpose: Check whether a concrete tool input/output can preserve the intended source-derived Galaxy collection shape.
refs.trigger: When implementing concrete steps for source-derived File/list/paired/list:paired/list:list inputs or outputs.

refs.purpose: Carry the open-requirements ledger: read open entries bearing on this step's decisions, mark resolved the ones it closes, and append any new unmet need it surfaces.
refs.verification: Promote after a worked run shows entries this Mold appends or resolves are consumed downstream without re-derivation.

refs.purpose: Bind the abstract step against the deterministic tool summary manifest emitted upstream — read `parsed_tool` for ports/datatypes and `input_schemas.workflow_step_linked` for valid step `tool_state` shape.
In/out contract: the draft this Mold reads and mutates in place conforms to [[galaxy-workflow-draft]]. Cast bundles the JSON Schema alongside the [[draft-validate]] CLI checks.

produces.description: gxformat2 skeleton with one more abstract step replaced by a concrete tool step (loop iteration output).
Ledger ([[open-requirements-ledger]]) with a blocking entry appended when a step's declared output can't be computed from its wired inputs; drives the fall-through to topology repair.

consumes.description: Galaxy tool summary manifest from [[summarize-galaxy-tool]] conforming to [[galaxy-tool-summary]]; binds the abstract step to a concrete tool's ports via the embedded `parsed_tool` and generated `input_schemas`.
gxformat2 skeleton being filled in step by step; the step replaces a placeholder in this draft.
Carried obligations ledger [[open-requirements-ledger]]: read entries the design tier recorded against this step before implementing; append a blocking entry if its output can't be computed.
